propositions: Measure how much a candidate gate change (e.g. R1 finder-rubric edit) perturbs verdicts of ~200 historical plans that already passed and closed cleanly, via counterfactual replay.
Control recall contamination by cloning the ticket store and deleting all *-REVIEW_RESULT.json files so prior_concerns/recall becomes a no-op while real-checkout code grounding is preserved.

Control baseline drift and LLM nondeterminism by replaying both the candidate and current/main gates fresh over the same recall-stripped clones instead of comparing against the persisted sidecar baseline.
Use a two-tier design: a coarse ~200-ticket transition matrix with a PASS→BLOCK flip-rate ≤0.02 bar, and a precise 30-plan FP-control gate replicated N=3.
Exclude INDETERMINATE verdicts and material-fingerprint mismatches from denominators, and over-sample a 50-ticket pool to yield 30 fully-usable tickets under the all-6-runs drop rule.

Decide R1 with two-level-median metrics: fail on blocking only if new_blocking_median > max(3, null_floor) and fail on advisories if new_advisory_median_delta > 1, matching findings by criteria-free tkey.
